filename: HGSC tissue dissociation for iPDC culture and flowcytometry_2023-10-03update.docx
relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Tissue dissociation, organoids and iPDCs/HGSC tissue dissociation for iPDC culture and flowcytometry_2023-10-03update.docx
full_text:
Protocol for HGSC tissue dissociation

Materials:

All the plastics are in the cupboard below the water bath or in the drawer next to laminar

Laminar hood

370C rotating incubator

Sterile surgical tools and scalpel

10 cm tissue culture plates

Histology cassettes

70 µm cell strainers

50 mL falcon tubes

1.5 mL / 2 mL Eppendorf tubes

70 cell strainer, cat#22-363-548 FischerScientific

Cryovials

Cell freezing box

Liquid nitrogen

37-41% Formaldehyde

DMEM F12, cat# 11320-074 Thermo Scientific: Cell culture fridge or cold room

GlutaMAX™-I (100X), cat#35050-061, GibcoLifeTechnologies: Cell culture cupboard RT

Penicillin-Streptomycin (10,000 U/ml), cat#15140122 GibcoLifeTechnologies: Cell culture fridge

HEPES, cat# H0887 Sigma: Cell culture fridge

Dispase II, cat#D4693-1G Sigma: stock 50 U/mLin Färkkilä lab fridge lower drawer

RQ1-RNAse free DNase, cat#M610A Promega: Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box

RBC lysis buffer, cat #11814389001 Roche: cell culture fridge

Stem cell banker cat#11890F Amsbio: cell culture fridge

Tissue dissociation medium: DMEM/F12 (500 mL) + 1x Glutamax (5 mL of 100x)+ 10 mM HEPES (5 mL of 1 M)+ Penstrep (5 mL): Ready to use medium is in the cell culture fridge.

(Note! This DMEM/F12 is different from “Advanced DMEM/F12 (#12634-010)” which is used to make medium for organoid culture)

Organoid culture base medium II – 50ml aliquot:

Base medium I found in cell culture +4C is prepared beforehand from Advanced DMEM/F12 (#12634-010) + supplements

Base medium II containing cytokines, is freshly prepared from medium I on collection day as 50 ml aliquot in a falcon tube after collecting sample --> this is used for iPDC culture

Start date:

Preparation prior to sample collection

1. Bring ice, and switch on the 370C incubator (located in Färkkilä lab below the lab bench).

2. Prepare the laminar hood as follows: Clean the surface with 70% EtOH, stick the plastic bag on to the right-hand side glass wall for collecting the waste tissues and tips. Place the sterile forceps, scissors, scalpel, and 10 cm tissue culture plate inside the laminar.

3. Prepare 2% virkon🡪 add 2 tablets of virkon into 1 L bottle, fill up the bottle with 500ml tap water, write the date and your initials, and let it dissolve.

Protocol:

Sample ID:

1. Enzymatic dissociation:

1.1. In the ONCOSYS laminar hood, transfer the tissue into a 10 cm plate. If the tissue is from the omentum, remove the normal looking omentum surrounding the tumor tissue. If the tissue is ovary/adnexa, remove the capsule/surrounding normal tissue. Mince the tumor piece (1-2 cm depending on the size of the sample) into smaller pieces using a sterile scalpel and or scissors; make sure to finely chop the tissue - large pieces will interfere with the cell yield.

1.2. Transfer the minced tissue into a 50 mL falcon tube containing 9,700 uL DMEM/F12 (with HEPES, pen/strep and glutamax) + 200 µL of Dispase stock (50 U/mL) + 100 µL RNAse free DNase I (9.7 ml of the medium + 200ul of Dispase II + 100ul of DNAase). Reduce or increase the volume depending on the tissue size. Seal the lid with parafilm, and tape the tube inside a 370C rotating incubator for 30 min.

During incubation time do the following:

Transfer the Dispase and DNAase tubes back to their boxes.

Cool cell culture centrifuge to +4 0C.

Place dirty instruments in a plastic tip box near the sink; pour virkon on top.

REMOVE if there is a patient ID labels from the jar(s), tear them into tiny pieces and trash!

Pour virkon into the sample jar, and leave them for 2-3 hr or until you finish with everything else. At the end of the day, pour the virkon waste to the sink, wash the instruments and the jar with tap water plus brush. Keep them for washing & autoclaving with a note for the cleaners. If there are large tissue pieces left after virkon decontamination, put it in a tissue paper and discard it in the plastic bag placed inside the hood.

Based on the calculation for the experiment set-up:

Take out the needed volume of omentum gel, aprotinin, thrombin, and fibrinogen from the freezer. Thaw everything else but fibrinogen on ice, which is thawed in a 37 0C water bath.

Prepare the needed volume of complete organoid medium:

Thaw the cytokines (IL2, IL15, IL7) on ice. Dilute IL-2-> 1 µL+ 999 µL of sterile 0.1 % BSA (in the cell culture fridge door).

For 10 mL aliquot of the organoid base medium, add:

76 uL of prediluted IL2, 10 uL IL15, 10 uL IL7, 10 uL ROCKi

Mix well and keep the tube inside the laminar until use

2. Filtration and centrifugation

2.1. At the end of incubation bring the tube to the laminar. Pre-label one 50 mL falcon with sample ID, remove the cap, place 70 µm cell strainer on top of 50 mL falcon, pre-wet strainer with 1 mL cold tissue dissociation medium. Transfer the digested suspensions through the strainer into 50 mL tubes and wash the strainers with 5 mL above cold tissue dissociation medium. If the strainer gets clogged, mix the tissue homogenate on top of the filter with a 1 mL tip.

3.2. Centrifuge 10 min @ 1000 rpm 4oC (Cell culture centrifuge).

NOTE: Always use cold DMEM/F12 medium - keep it on ice when not using.

3. Red blood cell lysis

3.1. Upon centrifugation from step 3.2., remove supernatant into a waste bottle containing virkon and resuspend the cell pellet in a 2 mL- 4 mL RBC lysis buffer depending on the pellet size. Incubate at room temperature (RT) for 5 min. Add 10 mL - 20 mL of cold tissue dissociation medium, mix well with the same pipette. Centrifuge 10 min @ 1000 rpm 4oC (Cell culture centrifuge).

4. Resuspension of the cell pellet viability measurement and downstream analysis

4.1. Carefully remove the supernatant into a waste bottle containing virkon, resuspend the cell pellet in 1-5 mL of iPDC culture medium.

NOTE: Decide the volume of resuspension depending on the pellet size. If the pellet is very large, the resuspension volume can be up to 5 mL.

5. Viability analysis using Trypan Blue:

5.1. Pipet 10 µL of cell suspension into a 1.5 mL tube and add 10 µL of trypan blue mix well. Pipette 10 µL per side x 2 sides of the LUNA cell-counting chamber. Note down the cell count & % viability from each side and take the average.

Cell count using LUNA cell counter

5.2. Calculate the amount of cells needed for iPDC culture and flow cytometry analysis, pipette the appropriate volume of cells into sterile 1.5 mL tube.

Proceed with the following step after setting up the culture: Once the iPDC culture is set up and the cells and matrix look OK, and the cells have been taken for flow cytometry. add 5 mL of tissue dissociating medium to the remaining cell suspension, centrifuge 5 min @ 1000 rpm 4oC (Cell culture centrifuge). Aspirate the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 1-5 mL of stem cell banker (1-10x1060C.

NOTE: First resuspend the cell pellet using 1 mL stem cell baker with 1 mL tip, mix well, then add remaining 4 mL, and mix again and aliquot to cryotubes.

Average Total_ cells_per_
mL | %_viability | Average_size_um | Volume_mL
* 5 (ONLY if you have 5 ml) | Dilution *10 (ONLY if you dilute) | Total cells
